time,\\n125:                              TransactionManager transactionManager,\\n126:                              BufferPool bufferPool) {\\n127:         this.logContext = logContext;\\n128:         this.log = logContext.logger(RecordAccumulator.class);\\n129:         this.closed = false;\\n130:         this.flushesInProgress = new AtomicInteger(0);\\n131:         this.appendsInProgress = new AtomicInteger(0);\\n132:         this.batchSize = batchSize;\\n133:         this.compression = compression;\\n134:         this.lingerMs = lingerMs;\\n135:         this.retryBackoff = new ExponentialBackoff(retryBackoffMs,\\n136:                 CommonClientConfigs.RETRY_BACKOFF_EXP_BASE,\\n137:                 retryBackoffMaxMs,\\n138:                 CommonClientConfigs.RETRY_BACKOFF_JITTER);\\n139:         this.deliveryTimeoutMs = deliveryTimeoutMs;\\n140:         this.enableAdaptivePartitioning = partitionerConfig.enableAdaptivePartitioning;\\n141:         this.partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs = partitionerConfig.partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs;\\n142:         this.free = bufferPool;\\n143:         this.incomplete = new IncompleteBatches();\\n144:         this.muted = new HashSet<>();\\n145:         this.time = time;\\n146:         nodesDrainIndex = new HashMap<>();\\n147:         this.transactionManager = transactionManager;\\n148:         registerMetrics(metrics, metricGrpName);\\n149:     }\\n150: \\n151:     /**\\n152:      * Create a new record accumulator with default partitioner config\\n153:      *\\n154:      * @param logContext The log context used for logging\\n155:      * @param batchSize The size to use when allocating {@link MemoryRecords} instances\\n156:      * @param compression The compression codec for the records\\n157:      * @param lingerMs An artificial delay time to add before declaring a records instance that isn't full ready for\\n158:      *        sending. This allows time for more records to arrive. Setting a non-zero lingerMs will trade off some\\n159:      *        latency for potentially better throughput due to more batching (and hence fewer, larger requests).\\n160:      * @param retryBackoffMs An artificial delay time to retry the produce request upon receiving an error. This avoids\\n161:      *        exhausting all retries in a short period of time.\\n162:      * @param retryBackoffMaxMs The upper bound of the retry backoff time.\\n163:      * @param deliveryTimeoutMs An upper bound on the time to report success or failure on record delivery\\n164:      * @param metrics The metrics\\n165:      * @param metricGrpName The metric group name\\n166:      * @param time The time instance to use\\n167:      * @param transactionManager The shared transaction state object which tracks producer IDs, epochs, and sequence\\n168:      *                           numbers per partition.\\n169:      * @param bufferPool The buffer pool\\n170:      */\\n171:     public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\\n172:                              int batchSize,\\n173:                              Compression compression,\\n174:                              int lingerMs,\\n175:                              long retryBackoffMs,\\n176:                              long retryBackoffMaxMs,\\n177:                              int deliveryTimeoutMs,\\n178:                              Metrics metrics,\\n179:                              String metricGrpName,\\n180:                              Time time,\\n181:                              TransactionManager transactionManager,\\n182:                              BufferPool bufferPool) {\\n183:         this(logContext,\\n184:             batchSize,\\n185:             compression,\\n186:             lingerMs,\\n187:             retryBackoffMs,\\n188:             retryBackoffMaxMs,\\n189:             deliveryTimeoutMs,\\n190:             new PartitionerConfig(),\\n191:             metrics,\\n192:             metricGrpName,\\n193:             time,\\n194:             transactionManager,\\n195:             bufferPool);\\n196:     }\\n197: \\n198:     private void registerMetrics(Metrics metrics, String metricGrpName) {\\n199:         metrics.addMetric(\\n200:             metrics.metricName(\\\"waiting-threads\\\", metricGrpName,\\n201:                 \\\"The number of user threads blocked waiting for buffer memory to enqueue their records\\\"),\\n202:             (config, now) -> free.queued());\\n203: \\n204:         metrics.addMetric(\\n205:             metrics.metricName(\\\"buffer-total-bytes\\\", metricGrpName,\\n206:                 \\\"The maximum amount of buffer memory the client can use (whether or not it is currently used).\\\"),\\n207:             (config, now) -> free.totalMemory());\\n208: \\n209:         metrics.addMetric(\\n210:             metrics.metricName(\\\"buffer-available-bytes\\\", metricGrpName,\\n211:                 \\\"The total amount of buffer memory that is not being used (either unallocated or in the free list).\\\"),\\n212:             (config, now) -> free.availableMemory());\\n213:     }\\n214: \\n215:     private void setPartition(AppendCallbacks callbacks, int partition) {\\n216:         if (callbacks != null)\\n217:             callbacks.setPartition(partition);\\n218:     }\\n219: \\n220:     /**\\n221:      * Check if partition concurrently changed, or we need to complete previously disabled partition change.\\n222:      *\\n223:      * @param topic The topic\\n224:      * @param topicInfo The topic info\\n225:      * @param partitionInfo The built-in partitioner's partition info\\n226:      * @param deque The partition queue\\n227:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\\n228:      * @param cluster THe cluster metadata\\n229:      * @return 'true' if partition changed and we need to get new partition info and retry,\\n230:      *         'false' otherwise\\n231:      */\\n232:     private boolean partitionChanged(String topic,\\n233:                                      TopicInfo topicInfo,\\n234:                                      BuiltInPartitioner.StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo,\\n235:                                      Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs,\\n236:                                      Cluster cluster) {\\n237:         if (topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.isPartitionChanged(partitionInfo)) {\\n238:             log.trace(\\\"Partition {} for topic {} switched by a concurrent append, retrying\\\",\\n239:                     partitionInfo.partition(), topic);\\n240:             return true;\\n241:         }\\n242: \\n243:         // We might have disabled partition switch if the queue had incomplete batches.\\n244:         // Check if all batches are full now and switch .\\n245:         if (allBatchesFull(deque)) {\\n246:             topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, 0, cluster, true);\\n247:             if (topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.isPartitionChanged(partitionInfo)) {\\n248:                 log.trace(\\\"Completed previously disabled switch for topic {} partition {}, retrying\\\",\\n249:                         topic, partitionInfo.partition());\\n250:                 return true;\\n251:             }\\n252:         }\\n253: \\n254:         return false;\\n255:     }\\n256: \\n257:     /**\\n258:      * Add a record to the accumulator, return the append result\\n259:      * <p>\\n260:      * The append result will contain the future metadata, and flag for whether the appended batch is full or a new batch is created\\n261:      * <p>\\n262:      *\\n263:      * @param topic The topic to which this record is being sent\\n264:      * @param partition The partition to which this record is being sent or RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION\\n265:      *                  if any partition could be used\\n266:      * @param timestamp The timestamp of the record\\n267:      * @param key The key for the record\\n268:      * @param value The value for the record\\n269:      * @param headers the Headers for the record\\n270:      * @param callbacks The callbacks to execute\\n271:      * @param maxTimeToBlock The maximum time in milliseconds to block for buffer memory to be available\\n272:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\\n273:      * @param cluster The cluster metadata\\n274:      */\\n275:     public RecordAppendResult append(String topic,\\n276:                                      int partition,\\n277:                                      long timestamp,\\n278:                                      byte[] key,\\n279:                                      byte[] value,\\n280:                                      Header[] headers,\\n281:                                      AppendCallbacks callbacks,\\n282:                                      long maxTimeToBlock,\\n283:                                      long nowMs,\\n284:                                      Cluster cluster) throws InterruptedException {\\n285:         TopicInfo topicInfo = topicInfoMap.computeIfAbsent(topic, k -> new TopicInfo(createBuiltInPartitioner(logContext, k, batchSize)));\\n286: \\n287:         // We keep track of the number of appending thread to make sure we do not miss batches in\\n288:         // abortIncompleteBatches().\\n289:         appendsInProgress.incrementAndGet();\\n290:         ByteBuffer buffer = null;\\n291:         if (headers == null) headers = Record.EMPTY_HEADERS;\\n292:         try {\\n293:             // Loop to retry in case we encounter partitioner's race conditions.\\n294:             while (true) {\\n295:                 // If the message doesn't have any partition affinity, so we pick a partition based on the broker\\n296:                 // availability and performance.  Note, that here we peek current partition before we hold the\\n297:                 // deque lock, so we'll need to make sure that it's not changed while we were waiting for the\\n298:                 // deque lock.\\n299:                 final BuiltInPartitioner.StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo;\\n300:                 final int effectivePartition;\\n301:                 if (partition == RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION) {\\n302:                     partitionInfo = topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.peekCurrentPartitionInfo(cluster);\\n303:                     effectivePartition = partitionInfo.partition();\\n304:                 } else {\\n305:                     partitionInfo = null;\\n306:                     effectivePartition = partition;\\n307:                 }\\n308: \\n309:                 // Now that we know the effective partition, let the caller know.\\n310:                 setPartition(callbacks, effectivePartition);\\n311: \\n312:                 // check if we have an in-progress batch\\n313:                 Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = topicInfo.batches.computeIfAbsent(effectivePartition, k -> new ArrayDeque<>());\\n314:                 synchronized (dq) {\\n315:                     // After taking the lock, validate that the partition hasn't changed and retry.\\n316:                     if (partitionChanged(topic, topicInfo, partitionInfo, dq, nowMs, cluster))\\n317:                         continue;\\n318: \\n319:                     RecordAppendResult appendResult = tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, dq, nowMs);\\n320:                     if (appendResult != null) {\\n321:                         // If queue has incomplete batches we disable switch (see comments in updatePartitionInfo).\\n322:                         boolean enableSwitch = allBatchesFull(dq);\\n323:                         topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, appendResult.appendedBytes, cluster, enableSwitch);\\n324:                         return appendResult;\\n325:                     }\\n326:                 }\\n327: \\n328:                 if (buffer == null) {\\n329:                     int size = Math.max(this.batchSize, AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(\\n330:                             RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression.type(), key, value, headers));\\n331:                     log.trace(\\\"Allocating a new {} byte message buffer for topic {} partition {} with remaining timeout {}ms\\\", size, topic, effectivePartition, maxTimeToBlock);\\n332:                     // This call may block if we exhausted buffer space.\\n333:                     buffer = free.allocate(size, maxTimeToBlock);\\n334:                     // Update the current time in case the buffer allocation blocked above.\\n335:                     // NOTE: getting time may be expensive, so calling it under a lock\\n336:                     // should be avoided.\\n337:                     nowMs = time.milliseconds();\\n338:                 }\\n339: \\n340:                 synchronized (dq) {\\n341:                     // After taking the lock, validate that the partition hasn't changed and retry.\\n342:                     if (partitionChanged(topic, topicInfo, partitionInfo, dq, nowMs, cluster))\\n343:                         continue;\\n344: \\n345:                     RecordAppendResult appendResult = appendNewBatch(topic, effectivePartition, dq, timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, buffer, nowMs);\\n346:                     // Set buffer to null, so that deallocate doesn't return it back to free pool, since it's used in the batch.\\n347:                     if (appendResult.newBatchCreated)\\n348:                         buffer = null;\\n349:                     // If queue has incomplete batches we disable switch (see comments in updatePartitionInfo).\\n350:                     boolean enableSwitch = allBatchesFull(dq);\\n351:                     topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, appendResult.appendedBytes, cluster, enableSwitch);\\n352:                     return appendResult;\\n353:                 }\\n354:             }\\n355:         } finally {\\n356:             free.deallocate(buffer);\\n357:             appendsInProgress.decrementAndGet();\\n358:         }\\n359:     }\\n360: \\n361:     /**\\n362:      * Append a new batch to the queue\\n363:      *\\n364:      * @param topic The topic\\n365:      * @param partition The partition (cannot be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION)\\n366:      * @param dq The queue\\n367:      * @param timestamp The timestamp of the record\\n368:      * @param key The key for the record\\n369:      * @param value The value for the record\\n370:      * @param headers the Headers for the record\\n371:      * @param callbacks The callbacks to execute\\n372:      * @param buffer The buffer for the new batch\\n373:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\\n374:      */\\n375:     private RecordAppendResult appendNewBatch(String topic,\\n376:                                               int partition,\\n377:                                               Deque<ProducerBatch> dq,\\n378:                                               long timestamp,\\n379:                                               byte[] key,\\n380:                                               byte[] value,\\n381:                                               Header[] headers,\\n382:                                               AppendCallbacks callbacks,\\n383:                                               ByteBuffer buffer,\\n384:                                               long nowMs) {\\n385:         assert partition != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\\n386: \\n387:         RecordAppendResult appendResult = tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, dq, nowMs);\\n388:         if (appendResult != null) {\\n389:             // Somebody else found us a batch, return the one we waited for! Hopefully this doesn't happen often...\\n390:             return appendResult;\\n391:         }\\n392: \\n393:         MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder = recordsBuilder(buffer);\\n394:         ProducerBatch batch = new ProducerBatch(new TopicPartition(topic, partition), recordsBuilder, nowMs);\\n395:         FutureRecordMetadata future = Objects.requireNonNull(batch.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers,\\n396:                 callbacks, nowMs));\\n397: \\n398:         dq.addLast(batch);\\n399:         incomplete.add(batch);\\n400: \\n401:         return new RecordAppendResult(future, dq.size() > 1 || batch.isFull(), true, batch.estimatedSizeInBytes());\\n402:     }\\n403: \\n404:     private MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder(ByteBuffer buffer) {\\n405:         return MemoryRecords.builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, 0L);\\n406:     }\\n407: \\n408:     /**\\n409:      * Check if all batches in the queue are full.\\n410:      */\\n411:     private boolean allBatchesFull(Deque<ProducerBatch> deque) {\\n412:         // Only the last batch may be incomplete, so we just check that.\\n413:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\\n414:         return last == null || last.isFull();\\n415:     }\\n416: \\n417:      /**\\n418:      *  Try to append to a ProducerBatch.\\n419:      *\\n420:      *  If it is full, we return null and a new batch is created. We also close the batch for record appends to free up\\n421:      *  resources like compression buffers. The batch will be fully closed (ie. the record batch headers will be written\\n422:      *  and memory records built) in one of the following cases (whichever comes first): right before send,\\n423:      *  if it is expired, or when the producer is closed.\\n424:      */\\n425:     private RecordAppendResult tryAppend(long timestamp, byte[] key, byte[] value, Header[] headers,\\n426:                                          Callback callback, Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs) {\\n427:         if (closed)\\n428:             throw new KafkaException(\\\"Producer closed while send in progress\\\");\\n429:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\\n430:         if (last != null) {\\n431:             int initialBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes();\\n432:             FutureRecordMetadata future = last.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callback, nowMs);\\n433:             if (future == null) {\\n434:                 last.closeForRecordAppends();\\n435:             } else {\\n436:                 int appendedBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes() - initialBytes;\\n437:                 return new RecordAppendResult(future, deque.size() > 1 || last.isFull(), false, appendedBytes);\\n438:             }\\n439:         }\\n440:         return null;\\n441:     }\\n442: \\n443:     private boolean isMuted(TopicPartition tp) {\\n444:         return muted.contains(tp);\\n445:     }\\n446: \\n447:     public void resetNextBatchExpiryTime() {\\n448:         nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n449:     }\\n450: \\n451:     public void maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(ProducerBatch batch) {\\n452:         if (batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs  > 0) {\\n453:             // the non-negative check is to guard us against potential overflow due to setting\\n454:             // a large value for deliveryTimeoutMs\\n455:             nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Math.min(nextBatchExpiryTimeMs, batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs);\\n456:         } else {\\n457:             log.warn(\\\"Skipping next batch expiry time update due to addition overflow: \\\"\\n458:                 + \\\"batch.createMs={}, deliveryTimeoutMs={}\\\", batch.createdMs, deliveryTimeoutMs);\\n459:         }\\n460:     }\\n461: \\n462:     /**\\n463:      * Get a list of batches which have been sitting in the accumulator too long and need to be expired.\\n464:      */\\n465:     public List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches(long now) {\\n466:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = new ArrayList<>();\\n467:         for (TopicInfo topicInfo : topicInfoMap.values()) {\\n468:             for (Deque<ProducerBatch> deque : topicInfo.batches.values()) {\\n469:                 // expire the batches in the order of sending\\n470:                 synchronized (deque) {\\n471:                     while (!deque.isEmpty()) {\\n472:                         ProducerBatch batch = deque.getFirst();\\n473:                         if (batch.hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(deliveryTimeoutMs, now)) {\\n474:                             deque.poll();\\n475:                             batch.abortRecordAppends();\\n476:                             expiredBatches.add(batch);\\n477:                         } else {\\n478:                             maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(batch);\\n479:                             break;\\n480:                         }\\n481:                     }\\n482:                 }\\n483:             }\\n484:         }\\n485:         return expiredBatches;\\n486:     }\\n487: \\n488:     public long getDeliveryTimeoutMs() {\\n489:         return deliveryTimeoutMs;\\n490:     }\\n491: \\n492:     /**\\n493:      * Re-enqueue the given record batch in the accumulator. In Sender.completeBatch method, we check\\n494:      * whether the batch has reached deliveryTimeoutMs or not. Hence we do not do the delivery timeout check here.\\n495:      */\\n496:     public void reenqueue(ProducerBatch batch, long now) {\\n497:         batch.reenqueued(now);\\n498:         Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = getOrCreateDeque(batch.topicPartition);\\n499:         synchronized (deque) {\\n500:             if (transactionManager != null)\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java%3FL1-500\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.clients.CommonClientConfigs;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.clients.MetadataSnapshot;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.Callback;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.RecordMetadata;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.Cluster;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.Node;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.AbstractRecords;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.CompressionRatioEstimator;\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.MemoryRecords;\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.MemoryRecordsBuilder;\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.Record;\n36: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.RecordBatch;\n37: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\n38: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CopyOnWriteMap;\n39: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ExponentialBackoff;\n40: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\n41: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ProducerIdAndEpoch;\n42: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n43: \n44: import org.slf4j.Logger;\n45: \n46: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\n47: import java.util.ArrayDeque;\n48: import java.util.ArrayList;\n49: import java.util.Collections;\n50: import java.util.Deque;\n51: import java.util.HashMap;\n52: import java.util.HashSet;\n53: import java.util.List;\n54: import java.util.Map;\n55: import java.util.Objects;\n56: import java.util.OptionalInt;\n57: import java.util.Set;\n58: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentMap;\n59: import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;\n60: \n61: /**\n62:  * This class acts as a queue that accumulates records into {@link MemoryRecords}\n63:  * instances to be sent to the server.\n64:  * <p>\n65:  * The accumulator uses a bounded amount of memory and append calls will block when that memory is exhausted, unless\n66:  * this behavior is explicitly disabled.\n67:  */\n68: public class RecordAccumulator {\n69: \n70:     private final LogContext logContext;\n71:     private final Logger log;\n72:     private volatile boolean closed;\n73:     private final AtomicInteger flushesInProgress;\n74:     private final AtomicInteger appendsInProgress;\n75:     private final int batchSize;\n76:     private final Compression compression;\n77:     private final int lingerMs;\n78:     private final ExponentialBackoff retryBackoff;\n79:     private final int deliveryTimeoutMs;\n80:     private final long partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs;  // latency threshold for marking partition temporary unavailable\n81:     private final boolean enableAdaptivePartitioning;\n82:     private final BufferPool free;\n83:     private final Time time;\n84:     private final ConcurrentMap<String /*topic*/, TopicInfo> topicInfoMap = new CopyOnWriteMap<>();\n85:     private final ConcurrentMap<Integer /*nodeId*/, NodeLatencyStats> nodeStats = new CopyOnWriteMap<>();\n86:     private final IncompleteBatches incomplete;\n87:     // The following variables are only accessed by the sender thread, so we don't need to protect them.\n88:     private final Set<TopicPartition> muted;\n89:     private final Map<String, Integer> nodesDrainIndex;\n90:     private final TransactionManager transactionManager;\n91:     private long nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Long.MAX_VALUE; // the earliest time (absolute) a batch will expire.\n92: \n93:     /**\n94:      * Create a new record accumulator\n95:      *\n96:      * @param logContext The log context used for logging\n97:      * @param batchSize The size to use when allocating {@link MemoryRecords} instances\n98:      * @param compression The compression codec for the records\n99:      * @param lingerMs An artificial delay time to add before declaring a records instance that isn't full ready for\n100:      *        sending. This allows time for more records to arrive. Setting a non-zero lingerMs will trade off some\n101:      *        latency for potentially better throughput due to more batching (and hence fewer, larger requests).\n102:      * @param retryBackoffMs An artificial delay time to retry the produce request upon receiving an error. This avoids\n103:      *        exhausting all retries in a short period of time.\n104:      * @param retryBackoffMaxMs The upper bound of the retry backoff time.\n105:      * @param deliveryTimeoutMs An upper bound on the time to report success or failure on record delivery\n106:      * @param partitionerConfig Partitioner config\n107:      * @param metrics The metrics\n108:      * @param metricGrpName The metric group name\n109:      * @param time The time instance to use\n110:      * @param transactionManager The shared transaction state object which tracks producer IDs, epochs, and sequence\n111:      *                           numbers per partition.\n112:      * @param bufferPool The buffer pool\n113:      */\n114:     public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n115:                              int batchSize,\n116:                              Compression compression,\n117:                              int lingerMs,\n118:                              long retryBackoffMs,\n119:                              long retryBackoffMaxMs,\n120:                              int deliveryTimeoutMs,\n121:                              PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig,\n122:                              Metrics metrics,\n123:                              String metricGrpName,\n124:                              Time time,\n125:                              TransactionManager transactionManager,\n126:                              BufferPool bufferPool) {\n127:         this.logContext = logContext;\n128:         this.log = logContext.logger(RecordAccumulator.class);\n129:         this.closed = false;\n130:         this.flushesInProgress = new AtomicInteger(0);\n131:         this.appendsInProgress = new AtomicInteger(0);\n132:         this.batchSize = batchSize;\n133:         this.compression = compression;\n134:         this.lingerMs = lingerMs;\n135:         this.retryBackoff = new ExponentialBackoff(retryBackoffMs,\n136:                 CommonClientConfigs.RETRY_BACKOFF_EXP_BASE,\n137:                 retryBackoffMaxMs,\n138:                 CommonClientConfigs.RETRY_BACKOFF_JITTER);\n139:         this.deliveryTimeoutMs = deliveryTimeoutMs;\n140:         this.enableAdaptivePartitioning = partitionerConfig.enableAdaptivePartitioning;\n141:         this.partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs = partitionerConfig.partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs;\n142:         this.free = bufferPool;\n143:         this.incomplete = new IncompleteBatches();\n144:         this.muted = new HashSet<>();\n145:         this.time = time;\n146:         nodesDrainIndex = new HashMap<>();\n147:         this.transactionManager = transactionManager;\n148:         registerMetrics(metrics, metricGrpName);\n149:     }\n150: \n151:     /**\n152:      * Create a new record accumulator with default partitioner config\n153:      *\n154:      * @param logContext The log context used for logging\n155:      * @param batchSize The size to use when allocating {@link MemoryRecords} instances\n156:      * @param compression The compression codec for the records\n157:      * @param lingerMs An artificial delay time to add before declaring a records instance that isn't full ready for\n158:      *        sending. This allows time for more records to arrive. Setting a non-zero lingerMs will trade off some\n159:      *        latency for potentially better throughput due to more batching (and hence fewer, larger requests).\n160:      * @param retryBackoffMs An artificial delay time to retry the produce request upon receiving an error. This avoids\n161:      *        exhausting all retries in a short period of time.\n162:      * @param retryBackoffMaxMs The upper bound of the retry backoff time.\n163:      * @param deliveryTimeoutMs An upper bound on the time to report success or failure on record delivery\n164:      * @param metrics The metrics\n165:      * @param metricGrpName The metric group name\n166:      * @param time The time instance to use\n167:      * @param transactionManager The shared transaction state object which tracks producer IDs, epochs, and sequence\n168:      *                           numbers per partition.\n169:      * @param bufferPool The buffer pool\n170:      */\n171:     public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n172:                              int batchSize,\n173:                              Compression compression,\n174:                              int lingerMs,\n175:                              long retryBackoffMs,\n176:                              long retryBackoffMaxMs,\n177:                              int deliveryTimeoutMs,\n178:                              Metrics metrics,\n179:                              String metricGrpName,\n180:                              Time time,\n181:                              TransactionManager transactionManager,\n182:                              BufferPool bufferPool) {\n183:         this(logContext,\n184:             batchSize,\n185:             compression,\n186:             lingerMs,\n187:             retryBackoffMs,\n188:             retryBackoffMaxMs,\n189:             deliveryTimeoutMs,\n190:             new PartitionerConfig(),\n191:             metrics,\n192:             metricGrpName,\n193:             time,\n194:             transactionManager,\n195:             bufferPool);\n196:     }\n197: \n198:     private void registerMetrics(Metrics metrics, String metricGrpName) {\n199:         metrics.addMetric(\n200:             metrics.metricName(\"waiting-threads\", metricGrpName,\n201:                 \"The number of user threads blocked waiting for buffer memory to enqueue their records\"),\n202:             (config, now) -> free.queued());\n203: \n204:         metrics.addMetric(\n205:             metrics.metricName(\"buffer-total-bytes\", metricGrpName,\n206:                 \"The maximum amount of buffer memory the client can use (whether or not it is currently used).\"),\n207:             (config, now) -> free.totalMemory());\n208: \n209:         metrics.addMetric(\n210:             metrics.metricName(\"buffer-available-bytes\", metricGrpName,\n211:                 \"The total amount of buffer memory that is not being used (either unallocated or in the free list).\"),\n212:             (config, now) -> free.availableMemory());\n213:     }\n214: \n215:     private void setPartition(AppendCallbacks callbacks, int partition) {\n216:         if (callbacks != null)\n217:             callbacks.setPartition(partition);\n218:     }\n219: \n220:     /**\n221:      * Check if partition concurrently changed, or we need to complete previously disabled partition change.\n222:      *\n223:      * @param topic The topic\n224:      * @param topicInfo The topic info\n225:      * @param partitionInfo The built-in partitioner's partition info\n226:      * @param deque The partition queue\n227:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\n228:      * @param cluster THe cluster metadata\n229:      * @return 'true' if partition changed and we need to get new partition info and retry,\n230:      *         'false' otherwise\n231:      */\n232:     private boolean partitionChanged(String topic,\n233:                                      TopicInfo topicInfo,\n234:                                      BuiltInPartitioner.StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo,\n235:                                      Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs,\n236:                                      Cluster cluster) {\n237:         if (topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.isPartitionChanged(partitionInfo)) {\n238:             log.trace(\"Partition {} for topic {} switched by a concurrent append, retrying\",\n239:                     partitionInfo.partition(), topic);\n240:             return true;\n241:         }\n242: \n243:         // We might have disabled partition switch if the queue had incomplete batches.\n244:         // Check if all batches are full now and switch .\n245:         if (allBatchesFull(deque)) {\n246:             topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, 0, cluster, true);\n247:             if (topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.isPartitionChanged(partitionInfo)) {\n248:                 log.trace(\"Completed previously disabled switch for topic {} partition {}, retrying\",\n249:                         topic, partitionInfo.partition());\n250:                 return true;\n251:             }\n252:         }\n253: \n254:         return false;\n255:     }\n256: \n257:     /**\n258:      * Add a record to the accumulator, return the append result\n259:      * <p>\n260:      * The append result will contain the future metadata, and flag for whether the appended batch is full or a new batch is created\n261:      * <p>\n262:      *\n263:      * @param topic The topic to which this record is being sent\n264:      * @param partition The partition to which this record is being sent or RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION\n265:      *                  if any partition could be used\n266:      * @param timestamp The timestamp of the record\n267:      * @param key The key for the record\n268:      * @param value The value for the record\n269:      * @param headers the Headers for the record\n270:      * @param callbacks The callbacks to execute\n271:      * @param maxTimeToBlock The maximum time in milliseconds to block for buffer memory to be available\n272:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\n273:      * @param cluster The cluster metadata\n274:      */\n275:     public RecordAppendResult append(String topic,\n276:                                      int partition,\n277:                                      long timestamp,\n278:                                      byte[] key,\n279:                                      byte[] value,\n280:                                      Header[] headers,\n281:                                      AppendCallbacks callbacks,\n282:                                      long maxTimeToBlock,\n283:                                      long nowMs,\n284:                                      Cluster cluster) throws InterruptedException {\n285:         TopicInfo topicInfo = topicInfoMap.computeIfAbsent(topic, k -> new TopicInfo(createBuiltInPartitioner(logContext, k, batchSize)));\n286: \n287:         // We keep track of the number of appending thread to make sure we do not miss batches in\n288:         // abortIncompleteBatches().\n289:         appendsInProgress.incrementAndGet();\n290:         ByteBuffer buffer = null;\n291:         if (headers == null) headers = Record.EMPTY_HEADERS;\n292:         try {\n293:             // Loop to retry in case we encounter partitioner's race conditions.\n294:             while (true) {\n295:                 // If the message doesn't have any partition affinity, so we pick a partition based on the broker\n296:                 // availability and performance.  Note, that here we peek current partition before we hold the\n297:                 // deque lock, so we'll need to make sure that it's not changed while we were waiting for the\n298:                 // deque lock.\n299:                 final BuiltInPartitioner.StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo;\n300:                 final int effectivePartition;\n301:                 if (partition == RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION) {\n302:                     partitionInfo = topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.peekCurrentPartitionInfo(cluster);\n303:                     effectivePartition = partitionInfo.partition();\n304:                 } else {\n305:                     partitionInfo = null;\n306:                     effectivePartition = partition;\n307:                 }\n308: \n309:                 // Now that we know the effective partition, let the caller know.\n310:                 setPartition(callbacks, effectivePartition);\n311: \n312:                 // check if we have an in-progress batch\n313:                 Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = topicInfo.batches.computeIfAbsent(effectivePartition, k -> new ArrayDeque<>());\n314:                 synchronized (dq) {\n315:                     // After taking the lock, validate that the partition hasn't changed and retry.\n316:                     if (partitionChanged(topic, topicInfo, partitionInfo, dq, nowMs, cluster))\n317:                         continue;\n318: \n319:                     RecordAppendResult appendResult = tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, dq, nowMs);\n320:                     if (appendResult != null) {\n321:                         // If queue has incomplete batches we disable switch (see comments in updatePartitionInfo).\n322:                         boolean enableSwitch = allBatchesFull(dq);\n323:                         topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, appendResult.appendedBytes, cluster, enableSwitch);\n324:                         return appendResult;\n325:                     }\n326:                 }\n327: \n328:                 if (buffer == null) {\n329:                     int size = Math.max(this.batchSize, AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(\n330:                             RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression.type(), key, value, headers));\n331:                     log.trace(\"Allocating a new {} byte message buffer for topic {} partition {} with remaining timeout {}ms\", size, topic, effectivePartition, maxTimeToBlock);\n332:                     // This call may block if we exhausted buffer space.\n333:                     buffer = free.allocate(size, maxTimeToBlock);\n334:                     // Update the current time in case the buffer allocation blocked above.\n335:                     // NOTE: getting time may be expensive, so calling it under a lock\n336:                     // should be avoided.\n337:                     nowMs = time.milliseconds();\n338:                 }\n339: \n340:                 synchronized (dq) {\n341:                     // After taking the lock, validate that the partition hasn't changed and retry.\n342:                     if (partitionChanged(topic, topicInfo, partitionInfo, dq, nowMs, cluster))\n343:                         continue;\n344: \n345:                     RecordAppendResult appendResult = appendNewBatch(topic, effectivePartition, dq, timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, buffer, nowMs);\n346:                     // Set buffer to null, so that deallocate doesn't return it back to free pool, since it's used in the batch.\n347:                     if (appendResult.newBatchCreated)\n348:                         buffer = null;\n349:                     // If queue has incomplete batches we disable switch (see comments in updatePartitionInfo).\n350:                     boolean enableSwitch = allBatchesFull(dq);\n351:                     topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, appendResult.appendedBytes, cluster, enableSwitch);\n352:                     return appendResult;\n353:                 }\n354:             }\n355:         } finally {\n356:             free.deallocate(buffer);\n357:             appendsInProgress.decrementAndGet();\n358:         }\n359:     }\n360: \n361:     /**\n362:      * Append a new batch to the queue\n363:      *\n364:      * @param topic The topic\n365:      * @param partition The partition (cannot be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION)\n366:      * @param dq The queue\n367:      * @param timestamp The timestamp of the record\n368:      * @param key The key for the record\n369:      * @param value The value for the record\n370:      * @param headers the Headers for the record\n371:      * @param callbacks The callbacks to execute\n372:      * @param buffer The buffer for the new batch\n373:      * @param nowMs The current time, in milliseconds\n374:      */\n375:     private RecordAppendResult appendNewBatch(String topic,\n376:                                               int partition,\n377:                                               Deque<ProducerBatch> dq,\n378:                                               long timestamp,\n379:                                               byte[] key,\n380:                                               byte[] value,\n381:                                               Header[] headers,\n382:                                               AppendCallbacks callbacks,\n383:                                               ByteBuffer buffer,\n384:                                               long nowMs) {\n385:         assert partition != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n386: \n387:         RecordAppendResult appendResult = tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, dq, nowMs);\n388:         if (appendResult != null) {\n389:             // Somebody else found us a batch, return the one we waited for! Hopefully this doesn't happen often...\n390:             return appendResult;\n391:         }\n392: \n393:         MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder = recordsBuilder(buffer);\n394:         ProducerBatch batch = new ProducerBatch(new TopicPartition(topic, partition), recordsBuilder, nowMs);\n395:         FutureRecordMetadata future = Objects.requireNonNull(batch.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers,\n396:                 callbacks, nowMs));\n397: \n398:         dq.addLast(batch);\n399:         incomplete.add(batch);\n400: \n401:         return new RecordAppendResult(future, dq.size() > 1 || batch.isFull(), true, batch.estimatedSizeInBytes());\n402:     }\n403: \n404:     private MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n405:         return MemoryRecords.builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, 0L);\n406:     }\n407: \n408:     /**\n409:      * Check if all batches in the queue are full.\n410:      */\n411:     private boolean allBatchesFull(Deque<ProducerBatch> deque) {\n412:         // Only the last batch may be incomplete, so we just check that.\n413:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\n414:         return last == null || last.isFull();\n415:     }\n416: \n417:      /**\n418:      *  Try to append to a ProducerBatch.\n419:      *\n420:      *  If it is full, we return null and a new batch is created. We also close the batch for record appends to free up\n421:      *  resources like compression buffers. The batch will be fully closed (ie. the record batch headers will be written\n422:      *  and memory records built) in one of the following cases (whichever comes first): right before send,\n423:      *  if it is expired, or when the producer is closed.\n424:      */\n425:     private RecordAppendResult tryAppend(long timestamp, byte[] key, byte[] value, Header[] headers,\n426:                                          Callback callback, Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs) {\n427:         if (closed)\n428:             throw new KafkaException(\"Producer closed while send in progress\");\n429:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\n430:         if (last != null) {\n431:             int initialBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes();\n432:             FutureRecordMetadata future = last.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callback, nowMs);\n433:             if (future == null) {\n434:                 last.closeForRecordAppends();\n435:             } else {\n436:                 int appendedBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes() - initialBytes;\n437:                 return new RecordAppendResult(future, deque.size() > 1 || last.isFull(), false, appendedBytes);\n438:             }\n439:         }\n440:         return null;\n441:     }\n442: \n443:     private boolean isMuted(TopicPartition tp) {\n444:         return muted.contains(tp);\n445:     }\n446: \n447:     public void resetNextBatchExpiryTime() {\n448:         nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n449:     }\n450: \n451:     public void maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(ProducerBatch batch) {\n452:         if (batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs  > 0) {\n453:             // the non-negative check is to guard us against potential overflow due to setting\n454:             // a large value for deliveryTimeoutMs\n455:             nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Math.min(nextBatchExpiryTimeMs, batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs);\n456:         } else {\n457:             log.warn(\"Skipping next batch expiry time update due to addition overflow: \"\n458:                 + \"batch.createMs={}, deliveryTimeoutMs={}\", batch.createdMs, deliveryTimeoutMs);\n459:         }\n460:     }\n461: \n462:     /**\n463:      * Get a list of batches which have been sitting in the accumulator too long and need to be expired.\n464:      */\n465:     public List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches(long now) {\n466:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = new ArrayList<>();\n467:         for (TopicInfo topicInfo : topicInfoMap.values()) {\n468:             for (Deque<ProducerBatch> deque : topicInfo.batches.values()) {\n469:                 // expire the batches in the order of sending\n470:                 synchronized (deque) {\n471:                     while (!deque.isEmpty()) {\n472:                         ProducerBatch batch = deque.getFirst();\n473:                         if (batch.hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(deliveryTimeoutMs, now)) {\n474:                             deque.poll();\n475:                             batch.abortRecordAppends();\n476:                             expiredBatches.add(batch);\n477:                         } else {\n478:                             maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(batch);\n479:                             break;\n480:                         }\n481:                     }\n482:                 }\n483:             }\n484:         }\n485:         return expiredBatches;\n486:     }\n487: \n488:     public long getDeliveryTimeoutMs() {\n489:         return deliveryTimeoutMs;\n490:     }\n491: \n492:     /**\n493:      * Re-enqueue the given record batch in the accumulator. In Sender.completeBatch method, we check\n494:      * whether the batch has reached deliveryTimeoutMs or not. Hence we do not do the delivery timeout check here.\n495:      */\n496:     public void reenqueue(ProducerBatch batch, long now) {\n497:         batch.reenqueued(now);\n498:         Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = getOrCreateDeque(batch.topicPartition);\n499:         synchronized (deque) {\n500:             if (transactionManager != null)\n501: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java%3FL1-500"
  }
}

Output
